name: New Valley Governorate (Egypt, 2006-2016)

colors.symbolism: the desert sands of the Western Desert in which the governorate's oases lie
the palm groves and oasis cultivation
underground water and the wave-like dune patterns of the desert

description: A white field bearing a central yellow triangular form representing a desert pyramid, charged with a green palm tree, with three blue wavy bands running across the lower portion of the flag.
history: Adopted in 2006 as part of Egypt's standardised governorate flag system, the design referenced the New Valley's defining features — the palm-bearing oases of Kharga, Dakhla, Farafra, and Bahariya set within the vast Western Desert. The flag was retired in 2016 when Egypt's governorate flags were redesigned in a new uniform format.

features.symbolism: the desert landscape of the New Valley

features.role: palm tree within the central yellow form
features.symbolism: the oasis agriculture of Kharga, Dakhla, Farafra, and Bahariya

features.role: stylized wave bands across the lower field
features.symbolism: the underground aquifers of the Western Desert and the dune ripples of the surrounding sands
